name: Nephilim Theory
description: The Nephilim Theory encompasses a range of interpretations surrounding the Nephilim, figures mentioned in the Hebrew Bible (Genesis 6:4) and other ancient texts. Proponents often interpret the Nephilim as the offspring of sexual unions between 'sons of God' (variously interpreted as fallen angels or descendants of Seth) and 'daughters of men.' These unions are believed to have resulted in a hybrid race of giants or exceptionally powerful beings who corrupted humanity. Some theories extend this interpretation to suggest the Nephilim influenced ancient civilizations, leaving behind megalithic structures and advanced technologies. Some even link them to UFO phenomena, considering them ancient extraterrestrial visitors. Mainstream scholarship generally views the Nephilim narrative as a mythological explanation for the origins of evil and violence in the world, or as a symbolic representation of the intermingling of different social groups. Biblical scholars often interpret the 'sons of God' as powerful human rulers who took multiple wives, leading to social inequality and moral decay. Archaeological evidence does not support the existence of a giant race of beings as described in some Nephilim theories. The dating of megalithic structures is also well-established through scientific methods and does not align with the timelines proposed by some proponents. Furthermore, the claim of advanced technology being a direct result of Nephilim influence lacks concrete evidence and relies heavily on speculative interpretations of ancient texts.
subtitle: Giants, Angels, and Ancient Mysteries

claims.claim: Nephilim were literal giants with immense strength.
claims.evidence: References to giants in Genesis 6:4 and other ancient texts; reports of giant skeletons found worldwide.
claims.criticism: Skeletal finds are often misidentified or hoaxes; biblical references are likely metaphorical or symbolic. Lack of verifiable archaeological evidence.

claims.claim: Nephilim possessed advanced technology and influenced ancient civilizations.
claims.evidence: Megalithic structures like Stonehenge and the pyramids; ancient texts describing advanced knowledge.
claims.criticism: Megalithic structures were built by humans using ingenious engineering techniques; alleged advanced knowledge can be attributed to human ingenuity and accumulated knowledge. Alternative explanations exist.

claims.claim: Nephilim are the ancestors of certain bloodlines or secret societies.
claims.evidence: Genealogical interpretations of biblical texts; claims of hidden knowledge passed down through specific groups.
claims.criticism: Lack of historical or genetic evidence to support bloodline claims; secret societies often involve symbolic or allegorical interpretations rather than literal descent from Nephilim.

keyFigures.name: Zecharia Sitchin
keyFigures.role: Popularized the idea of ancient alien influence on humanity, including Nephilim.

keyFigures.name: Michael S. Heiser
keyFigures.role: Biblical scholar who critiques the popular interpretations of Nephilim, offering alternative explanations.

keyFigures.name: L.A. Marzulli
keyFigures.role: Promoter of Nephilim theory, focusing on supposed archaeological evidence and biblical interpretations.

timeline.event: First mention of Nephilim in the Hebrew Bible.
Expands on the Nephilim narrative, describing their origins and actions.
Rise of alternative history and ancient alien theories, leading to renewed interest in the Nephilim.

sources.author: Michael S. Heiser
sources.work: The Unseen Realm: Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the Bible
sources.title: Michael S. Heiser

sources.author: L.A. Marzulli
sources.work: On the Trail of the Nephilim
sources.title: L.A. Marzulli

variants: Sons of God and Daughters of Men
